pr.title: Added Fox item attributes and values
pr.author: AaronB-Hub

summary: Closed, apparently unmerged PR that attempted to document and type item variable and special-attribute structs for Fox-related items, especially Fox laser/blaster/illusion and light-gun beam/ray data. The diff mostly replaces offset-style placeholder fields such as xDD4/xDD8/xDDC with semantic names like scale, angle, speed, pos, lifetime, and max_scale, updates Fox laser code to use those names, and annotates several attribute constants with observed values. There was no PR body, review discussion, or reviewer feedback in the provided slice, so intent beyond the diff/title is inferred only from code changes.
what_changed: Added FoxBlasterAttr, FoxLaserAttr, and FoxIllusionAttr structs in itCharItems.h with placeholder field names plus comments for some known indexed values.
Renamed FoxLaserVars fields from raw offsets xDD4/xDD8/xDDC/xDE0 to scale, angle, speed, and pos, while preserving offset comments mapping each field to ip+DD4 through ip+DE0.
Updated itfoxlaser.c to use FoxLaserAttr for specialAttributes instead of treating the item variable struct as attributes.

Updated Fox laser runtime code to use named item variables: scale for beam scale accumulation and JObj scale, angle for trigonometry and reflection normalization, speed for velocity calculations, and pos for collision/reference position storage.
Renamed the final parameter of it_8029C504 from arg9 to speed in both the C file and header.
Reorganized and annotated light-gun-related structs in itCommonItems.h: ItLGunVars, ItLGunAttr, ItLGunRayVars, ItLGunRayAttr, ItLGunBeamVars, and ItLGunBeamAttr.
Renamed ItLGunBeamAttr.x0 to lifetime and updated itlgunbeam.c to use item_spec_attr->lifetime for both the item var and it_80275158 timer setup.
Removed the ItCapsuleAttrs capsule member from the Item xDD4_itemVar union in types.h, though the diff excerpt does not explain why.
Dropped the parameter name ref from the it_8029CCF8 prototype, leaving only the HSD_GObj* type.
smart_moves: Separated per-item runtime variables from article special attributes: Fox laser spawn now reads x4_specialAttributes into a FoxLaserAttr pointer and writes runtime state into item->xDD4_itemVar.foxlaser.
Kept offset comments alongside semantic field names, which preserves decomp layout traceability while making callsites easier to read.

Used observed attribute values in comments, such as FoxLaserAttr lifetime [35], scale [3], ItLGunRayAttr speed [5], lifetime [80], max_scale [3], and ItLGunBeamAttr lifetime [18], making future data-table verification easier.
Propagated field renames through behavior code rather than only headers, reducing ambiguity about what each field controls.
Renaming arg9 to speed was justified by its direct use in velocity calculations with cosf/sinf.
decomp_lessons: When specialAttributes and itemVar structs share similar offset-looking fields, verify which memory region a pointer actually references before naming it; this PR corrected Fox laser code to distinguish FoxLaserAttr from FoxLaserVars.

Semantic names are strongest when anchored by usage: speed is multiplied by cosf/sinf, angle is normalized and passed to atan2-related logic, scale is clamped and passed to HSD_JObjSetScaleZ, and lifetime is passed to it_80275158.
For item variable structs inside xDD4_itemVar, retain ip+offset comments so future union layout work can audit byte positions without reverting to anonymous xDD4-style names.

Attribute structs can be partially named: fields with clear behavior can receive names like lifetime/max_scale while uncertain fields can remain x4/x8/xC with explanatory comments about their role in calculations.
Closed/unmerged PRs with no discussion should be treated as evidence of a proposed naming/layout direction, not necessarily accepted project convention.
naming_conventions: Runtime item variable structs use names ending in Vars, e.g. FoxLaserVars, ItLGunVars, ItLGunRayVars, ItLGunBeamVars.
Article/special-attribute structs use names ending in Attr, e.g. FoxLaserAttr, FoxBlasterAttr, FoxIllusionAttr, ItLGunRayAttr, ItLGunBeamAttr.
Known runtime fields should prefer semantic names such as scale, angle, speed, pos, timer, and lifetime over raw offset names.
Unconfirmed attribute fields can remain x0/x4/x8-style, ideally with comments indicating observed constants or calculation role.
Offset comments in item variable structs used the pattern /* +N ip+DDD */ to show both struct-relative and Item-plus-offset positions.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use dataflow from math calls to justify field names: trigonometric inputs identify angles, trigonometric-scaled values identify speeds, and HSD_JObjSetScaleZ arguments identify scale fields.
Use timer setup calls such as it_80275158 to identify lifetime attributes.
Use clamp targets to identify max scale or final scale attributes.
Preserve exact struct field order and sizes while renaming fields to avoid changing layout-sensitive matching behavior.
The diff did not include explicit matching-score discussion, asm diffs, or reviewer notes about generated code.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, reviews, or PR body were present in the provided slice.
Because the PR was closed with merged_at null and no visible discussion, there is no evidence here explaining why it was closed or whether any names were rejected.
searchable_terms: Added Fox item attributes and values
follow_up_queries: Check later merged PRs for accepted FoxLaserVars, FoxLaserAttr, and ItLGunBeamAttr naming to see whether this closed PR was superseded.
Search for removal or relocation of ItCapsuleAttrs capsule in the Item xDD4_itemVar union to understand the types.h deletion.
Compare current master definitions of Fox laser and light-gun structs against this PR's proposed field names and comments.
Look for data table references validating FoxLaserAttr lifetime [35], scale [3], and ItLGunBeamAttr angle constants.
